chunks.text: Joining EECS Domain | IRIS Skip to main content Skip to primary navigation Skip to primary sidebar Joining EECS Domain WHY JOIN YOUR MACINTOSH TO A WINDOWS DOMAIN? Allows for auto-mounting of your networked home directory. More Kerberos security. Sim

worked home directory. More Kerberos security. Simplifies access to other EECS Windows resources. Most MacOS folks do NOT join the EECS Windows domain, but you may find it useful if you are, for instance, an EECS staff or faculty member that frequent

nce, an EECS staff or faculty member that frequently accesses project file shares. BEFORE YOU JOIN THE DOMAIN: Please make sure your system is compliant with minimum campus security standards . In particular, make sure that your system has all update

ticular, make sure that your system has all updates applied from Software Update. When registering your system be sure to select “Yes” when asked to join the EECS domain. If local accounts are present on the host, please confirm they have unique name

on the host, please confirm they have unique names. If you already have a local account with the same name as your EECS Windows user name, you will need to follow other instructions to join your host to the domain. TO JOIN THE DOMAIN: Log in to your

to the domain. TO JOIN THE DOMAIN: Log in to your host (you will need administrator privileges to perform these tasks). Make sure you have a successful network connection, so that you can communicate with our domain controllers. Open the “Directory

with our domain controllers. Open the “Directory Utility” application in /System/Library/CoreServices/ In “Directory Utility,” click the lock to make changes, and authenticate as a local administrator. Check the box next to “Active Directory” and cl

r. Check the box next to “Active Directory” and click the little pencil icon to edit/configure the service. In the configuration window that opens, enter eecs.berkeley.edu for the Active Directory Domain and your Windows system name for the Computer

ain and your Windows system name for the Computer ID. Click Show Options. Fill in “User Experience” with all defaults selected (suggested, but optional). Fill in the “Administrative” tab, if you want to define Active Directory groups or users that wi

to define Active Directory groups or users that will have administrator privilege on the Mac. Use the + and – buttons to add or remove administrators. When you are done, click Bind. The “Network Administrator Required” window will open, fill in the i

istrator Required” window will open, fill in the information upon request. You will need a Windows username and password that has privilege to join this object to the EECS Windows Domain. You must also supply the DN of the OU the object is in (e.g. O

o supply the DN of the OU the object is in (e.g. OU=dept-computers,DC=eecs,DC=berkeley,DC=edu). Click OK when the information is supplied and you will see the bind steps 1–5 start to process. During the bind process, in step 4, you may be prompted to

he bind process, in step 4, you may be prompted to click OK to agree. If you are successful in binding to the EECS Windows Domain, steps 1–5 will be complete and the “Bind” button will changes to “Unbind.” Click OK to leave “Configuration” and return

ind.” Click OK to leave “Configuration” and return to “Directory Utility”. To complete access check the “Authentication” and “Contacts” categories by clicking the “Search Policy” icon at the top. If /Active Directory/All Domains is not present in bot

Active Directory/All Domains is not present in both, click Add to add them. Any changes made to “Directory Utility” have to be applied; to apply changes click Apply. Quit “Directory Utility”. Your Apple host is now an EECS Windows domain member. Log

le host is now an EECS Windows domain member. Log out and log back in using your EECS Windows username and password. Your home directory should mount automatically. Primary Sidebar IRIS Service Status We have 0 Active Incidents, and 1 Scheduled Maint
We have 0 Active Incidents, and 1 Scheduled Maintenance noted. IST Service Status Outages to campus services are listed at berkeley.statusdashboard.com .
